title: Routing, Selection, Translation, and Redundancy · Changed-Topology Lab
summary: Connect branch, headquarters, cloud, and internet routes while diagnosing asymmetry and failover. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a routed hybrid branch design with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Read connected, static, dynamic, default, and host routes and select the longest matching destination prefix.
Configure and evaluate static and default routes with next-hop reachability, recursion, failover, and documentation.
Compare BGP, EIGRP, and OSPF scope, neighbor behavior, path information, convergence, and use cases at N10-009 depth.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.instruction:

A defensible solution applies Net Routing Table Forwarding, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a routed hybrid branch design while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Routers choose the route with the lowest metric before comparing prefix length. This fails the Net Routing Table Forwarding contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: Zero Trust Architecture (NIST SP 800-207) — Controls zero-trust terminology, policy decision, trust, identity, and least-privilege coverage.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Read connected, static, dynamic, default, and host routes and select the longest matching destination prefix.
